title: Disable empty logrus timestamps to reduce logger noise
body:
The logrus timestmaps are always empty where we can disable them to have
cleaner log outputs:

Before:
```
DEBU[0000] [graphdriver] trying provided driver "overlay"
```

After:
```
DEBU [graphdriver] trying provided driver "overlay"
```

comments.body:
:pushpin: Commit 328bf5b has been approved by `rhatdan`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=rhatdan 328bf5be80444da8b81c38d7173090fd6f4b85bd -->
The timestamps are count of elapsed seconds since the command started running.

> The timestamps are count of elapsed seconds since the command started running.

Ah, this makes sense. Do we need those for a command line application? I'm not sure but feel free to close if you think they add valuable information to the user.
